findings: Hook (17 + 25 + 13) stages why rearranging matters before the formal property names arrive — students see the payoff first.
The trap slide (08) explicitly disarms the most common Algebra I error — treating subtraction and division like addition and multiplication.
Pause #1 tests identification of property names on three mixed lines — exactly the dashboard assignment skill.
Pause #2 requires the full simplification chain (distributive → commutative → associative → combine) and the answer-reveal slide labels each move.
Final path slide ends with a concrete Learn Portal action tied to the published assignment.
watch_points: Distributive slide (06) and identity/inverse slide (07) are visually denser than the others; keep narration pace measured during recording.
